hypothesis: Including explicit heating energy (to 1500°C at 60% furnace efficiency using tabulated heat capacities) and purification costs (estimated from ML-predicted solubilities/volatilities of byproducts) in the workflow will reverse the Ca2SiO4 recipe ranking, such that Ca3SiO5+SiO2 becomes at least 20% more cost-effective (grams per euro) than CaO+SiO2 for batch sizes ≥1000 kg when electricity prices are ≥0.12 €/kWh.
